001/*
002 * Licensed to the Apache Software Foundation (ASF) under one or more
003 * contributor license agreements.  See the NOTICE file distributed with
004 * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.
005 * The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0
006 * (the "License"); you may not use this file except in compliance with
007 * the License.  You may obtain a copy of the License at
008 *
009 *      http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
010 *
011 * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
012 * distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
013 * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
014 * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
015 * limitations under the License.
016 */
017package org.apache.juneau.commons.concurrent;
018
019import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.AssertionUtils.*;
020import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.Utils.*;
021
022import java.util.*;
023import java.util.concurrent.atomic.*;
024import java.util.function.*;
025
026/**
027 * A thread-safe reference that combines {@link AtomicReference} with {@link Optional}-like functionality.
028 *
029 * <p>
030 * This class extends {@link AtomicReference} to provide both atomic reference operations and Optional-like
031 * convenience methods for working with potentially null values. It is thread-safe and can be used in
032 * concurrent environments where you need to atomically update a reference while also having Optional-like
033 * operations.
034 *
035 * <h5 class='section'>Features:</h5>
036 * <ul class='spaced-list'>
037 *    <li>Extends AtomicReference - inherits all atomic operations (get, set, compareAndSet, etc.)
038 *    <li>Optional-like API - provides isPresent(), isEmpty(), map(), orElse(), etc.
039 *    <li>Thread-safe - all operations are atomic
040 *    <li>Null-safe - handles null values gracefully (null represents "empty")
041 * </ul>
042 *
043 * <h5 class='section'>Usage:</h5>
044 * <p class='bjava'>
045 *    <jc>// Create an empty reference</jc>
046 *    NullableReference&lt;String&gt; <jv>ref</jv> = NullableReference.<jsm>empty</jsm>();
047 *
048 *    <jc>// Set a value atomically</jc>
049 *    <jv>ref</jv>.set(<js>"value"</js>);
050 *
051 *    <jc>// Check if value is present</jc>
052 *    <jk>if</jk> (<jv>ref</jv>.isPresent()) {
053 *       String <jv>value</jv> = <jv>ref</jv>.get();
054 *    }
055 *
056 *    <jc>// Use Optional-like methods</jc>
057 *    String <jv>result</jv> = <jv>ref</jv>.map(String::toUpperCase).orElse(<js>"default"</js>);
058 *
059 *    <jc>// Atomic update</jc>
060 *    <jv>ref</jv>.compareAndSet(<js>"value"</js>, <js>"newValue"</js>);
061 * </p>
062 *
063 * <h5 class='section'>Thread Safety:</h5>
064 * <p>
065 * This class is thread-safe. All operations inherit thread-safety from {@link AtomicReference}.
066 * Multiple threads can safely call methods like {@link #get()}, {@link #set(Object)}, {@link #compareAndSet(Object, Object)},
067 * and the Optional-like methods concurrently.
068 *
069 * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
070 *    <li class='jc'>{@link AtomicReference} - Base class providing atomic operations
071 *    <li class='jc'>{@link Optional} - Java's Optional class
072 *    <li class='jc'>{@link org.apache.juneau.commons.function.NullableSupplier} - Similar functionality for suppliers
073 * </ul>
074 *
075 * @param <V> The type of value held by this reference.
076 */
077@SuppressWarnings({
078   "java:S115" // Constants use UPPER_snakeCase convention
079})
080public class NullableReference<V> extends AtomicReference<V> {
081
082   // Argument name constants for assertArgNotNull
083   private static final String ARG_action = "action";
084   private static final String ARG_emptyAction = "emptyAction";
085   private static final String ARG_exceptionSupplier = "exceptionSupplier";
086   private static final String ARG_mapper = "mapper";
087   private static final String ARG_other = "other";
088   private static final String ARG_predicate = "predicate";
089
090   private static final long serialVersionUID = 1L;
091
092   /**
093    * Creates an empty NullableReference with an initial value of <jk>null</jk>.
094    *
095    * @param <V> The value type.
096    * @return A new empty NullableReference.
097    */
098   public static <V> NullableReference<V> empty() {
099      return new NullableReference<>(null);
100   }
101
102   /**
103    * Creates an NullableReference with the specified initial value.
104    *
105    * @param <V> The value type.
106    * @param initialValue The initial value. Can be <jk>null</jk>.
107    * @return A new NullableReference with the specified value.
108    */
109   public static <V> NullableReference<V> of(V initialValue) {
110      return new NullableReference<>(initialValue);
111   }
112
113   /**
114    * Creates an NullableReference with the specified initial value (alias for {@link #of(Object)}).
115    *
116    * @param <V> The value type.
117    * @param initialValue The initial value. Can be <jk>null</jk>.
118    * @return A new NullableReference with the specified value.
119    */
120   public static <V> NullableReference<V> ofNullable(V initialValue) {
121      return new NullableReference<>(initialValue);
122   }
123
124   /**
125    * Constructor with initial value.
126    *
127    * @param initialValue The initial value. Can be <jk>null</jk>.
128    */
129   public NullableReference(V initialValue) {
130      super(initialValue);
131   }
132
133   /**
134    * Default constructor (initializes to <jk>null</jk>).
135    */
136   public NullableReference() {
137      super();
138   }
139
140   /**
141    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if the current value is not <jk>null</jk>.
142    *
143    * @return <jk>true</jk> if the current value is not <jk>null</jk>.
144    */
145   public boolean isPresent() {
146      return nn(get());
147   }
148
149   /**
150    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if the current value is <jk>null</jk>.
151    *
152    * @return <jk>true</jk> if the current value is <jk>null</jk>.
153    */
154   public boolean isEmpty() {
155      return !isPresent();
156   }
157
158   /**
159    * If a value is present, applies the provided mapping function to it and returns an NullableReference describing the result.
160    *
161    * @param <U> The type of the result of the mapping function.
162    * @param mapper A mapping function to apply to the value, if present. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
163    * @return An NullableReference describing the result of applying a mapping function to the value, if a value is present, otherwise an empty NullableReference.
164    */
165   public <U> NullableReference<U> map(Function<? super V, ? extends U> mapper) {
166      assertArgNotNull(ARG_mapper, mapper);
167      V value = get();
168      return nn(value) ? NullableReference.of(mapper.apply(value)) : NullableReference.empty();
169   }
170
171   /**
172    * If a value is present, returns the result of applying the given NullableReference-bearing mapping function to the value, otherwise returns an empty NullableReference.
173    *
174    * @param <U> The type parameter to the NullableReference returned by the mapping function.
175    * @param mapper A mapping function to apply to the value, if present. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
176    * @return The result of applying an NullableReference-bearing mapping function to the value, if a value is present, otherwise an empty NullableReference.
177    */
178   public <U> NullableReference<U> flatMap(Function<? super V, ? extends NullableReference<? extends U>> mapper) {
179      assertArgNotNull(ARG_mapper, mapper);
180      V value = get();
181      if (nn(value)) {
182         NullableReference<? extends U> result = mapper.apply(value);
183         return result != null ? NullableReference.ofNullable(result.get()) : NullableReference.empty();
184      }
185      return NullableReference.empty();
186   }
187
188   /**
189    * If a value is present, and the value matches the given predicate, returns an NullableReference describing the value, otherwise returns an empty NullableReference.
190    *
191    * @param predicate A predicate to apply to the value, if present. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
192    * @return An NullableReference describing the value if a value is present and the value matches the given predicate, otherwise an empty NullableReference.
193    */
194   public NullableReference<V> filter(Predicate<? super V> predicate) {
195      assertArgNotNull(ARG_predicate, predicate);
196      V value = get();
197      return (nn(value) && predicate.test(value)) ? NullableReference.of(value) : NullableReference.empty();
198   }
199
200   /**
201    * If a value is present, returns the value, otherwise returns <jk>other</jk>.
202    *
203    * @param other The value to be returned if there is no value present. Can be <jk>null</jk>.
204    * @return The value, if present, otherwise <jk>other</jk>.
205    */
206   public V orElse(V other) {
207      V value = get();
208      return nn(value) ? value : other;
209   }
210
211   /**
212    * If a value is present, returns the value, otherwise returns the result produced by the supplying function.
213    *
214    * @param other A {@link Supplier} whose result is returned if no value is present. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
215    * @return The value, if present, otherwise the result of <jk>other.get()</jk>.
216    */
217   public V orElseGet(Supplier<? extends V> other) {
218      assertArgNotNull(ARG_other, other);
219      V value = get();
220      return nn(value) ? value : other.get();
221   }
222
223   /**
224    * If a value is present, returns the value, otherwise throws an exception produced by the exception supplying function.
225    *
226    * @param <X> Type of the exception to be thrown.
227    * @param exceptionSupplier The supplying function that produces an exception to be thrown. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
228    * @return The value, if present.
229    * @throws X If no value is present.
230    */
231   public <X extends Throwable> V orElseThrow(Supplier<? extends X> exceptionSupplier) throws X {
232      assertArgNotNull(ARG_exceptionSupplier, exceptionSupplier);
233      V value = get();
234      if (nn(value))
235         return value;
236      throw exceptionSupplier.get();
237   }
238
239   /**
240    * If a value is present, performs the given action with the value, otherwise does nothing.
241    *
242    * @param action The action to be performed, if a value is present. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
243    */
244   public void ifPresent(Consumer<? super V> action) {
245      assertArgNotNull(ARG_action, action);
246      V value = get();
247      if (nn(value))
248         action.accept(value);
249   }
250
251   /**
252    * If a value is present, performs the given action with the value, otherwise performs the given empty-based action.
253    *
254    * @param action The action to be performed, if a value is present. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
255    * @param emptyAction The empty-based action to be performed, if no value is present. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
256    */
257   public void ifPresentOrElse(Consumer<? super V> action, Runnable emptyAction) {
258      assertArgNotNull(ARG_action, action);
259      assertArgNotNull(ARG_emptyAction, emptyAction);
260      V value = get();
261      if (nn(value))
262         action.accept(value);
263      else
264         emptyAction.run();
265   }
266
267   /**
268    * Converts this NullableReference to an {@link Optional}.
269    *
270    * @return An Optional containing the value if present, otherwise an empty Optional.
271    */
272   public Optional<V> toOptional() {
273      return opt(get());
274   }
275}
